facts.label: Edvar: Shadow-Walkers boarded a trawler
facts.desc: Edvar found the trawler, two figures matching SW description, heading toward a Lösweg vessel.

facts.label: West is closed — Cumbria sails at dawn
facts.desc: The party's return route is gone. Committed east.

facts.label: The Yngondi Wastes — named for the first time
facts.desc: Floki or Edvar names the eastern territory. Makes the destination real.

facts.label: Floki's grandmother's prophecy
facts.desc: "When they come from the east, the thing sleeping under the Yngondi Wastes will be close to waking."

facts.label: Six artefacts in the Greyback cargo
facts.desc: The cargo is real, old, and too important to be ordinary freight. Stormwatch-origin seals.

facts.label: The Maw — named, inland location
facts.desc: Floki's chart names the Maw: a deep gorge north of Torweg. The artefacts are pointed there.

facts.label: Scroll case is warm — reacts to the cargo
facts.desc: The scroll case brought from Halvash responds to proximity to the Greyback cargo. Not nothing.

facts.label: Carver's spiral matches bone disc geometry
facts.desc: Carvings on the crate braces use the same geometric language as the dead Shadow-Walker's markings.

facts.label: Deepwhale followed both crossings
facts.desc: Floki confirms it followed them east too. It recognised something aboard — not hunger, interest.

facts.label: Hag passes cargo north — Yngondi direction
facts.desc: The sea hag is a distribution point. Objects have been moving north on the Whale-Road for months.

facts.label: Thrall wears Lösweg-woven cloth
facts.desc: The thralled creature's clothing is Lösweg-made. Floki recognises the weave — says nothing for a moment.

facts.label: Maw agents smell — specific description
facts.desc: Wet stone, cold metal, and something else. The smell is consistent across all Maw-adjacent encounters.

facts.label: Ragnfridd receives the Oath-Ring of Veyr from Floki
facts.desc: Gifted before Torweg. It belonged to Floki's grandmother. Floki knows it is special but not why. "Some things are not owned. Only carried."

facts.label: Ragnfridd reads the inscription (privately)
facts.desc: "Hold what the sea returns. Name what the grave refuses. When the third silence breaks, let the witness speak." The last line is not advice. It is instruction.

facts.label: Torweg flag at half-mast
facts.desc: No one mentions why. It has been there three weeks. Nobody wants to be the one to ask.

facts.label: Welcome is wrong — too formal, too fast
facts.desc: The harbour welcome is organised and efficient. Halvash arrivals don't usually get this. Something was watching for them.

facts.label: Brynja's speech rhythms are wrong
facts.desc: She pauses in the wrong places. Corrects herself. She has been frightened for a while and is trying not to show it.

facts.label: Crow archives — records going back before the Calamity
facts.desc: The archive Brynja keeps is one of the oldest in Lösweg. Khôràna may have older. For Rowan.

facts.label: Fisherman's stitch — foreign pattern on local clothing
facts.desc: A sailor wearing Lösweg clothes but with an Issylran stitching pattern. Small detail, visible if looking.

facts.label: Black pin in the map at Ashfen's address
facts.desc: Brynja's informal map of "problems" has a black pin at an address near the south dock. Ashfen lives there.

facts.label: Brynja's record — family with no origin
facts.desc: A family appears in the Lösweg council record sixty years ago, fully formed. No prior documentation. This is Mathr's family.

facts.label: Brynja has seen one of the artefacts
facts.desc: She handled one briefly three months ago — brought to her for authentication. She said she didn't know it. She lied.

facts.label: Vane's south dock orders — Mathr's authority
facts.desc: The cargo movements at the south dock are authorised by Mathr's seal. Vane's signature is on the paperwork.

facts.label: King Dugweb's annual visit — seven weeks
facts.desc: Dugweb visits Lösweg annually. He is due in seven weeks. Mathr has managed these visits for sixty years.

facts.label: Jarl Beorn — trusts Mathr implicitly
facts.desc: Beorn's name comes up as the counter-weight. He is not corrupt — he is deceived. There is a path.

facts.label: Ashfen asks different questions now
facts.desc: Brix: "He doesn't remember old jokes. He asks different questions. Not all of him came back from Torweg."

facts.label: Ashfen arrested two fishermen — no reason given
facts.desc: Two fishermen he'd known for years. Held for four days. Released without explanation. Nobody asked.

facts.label: Maw operatives use specific tools — saw-marks
facts.desc: The crates at the south dock show tool-marks consistent with the hag's operation and the Greyback cargo unpacking.

facts.label: Aeorian note — Ashvein reference
facts.desc: A note found in Duvash's materials references Aeorian glass and specifically names Ashvein. Xyrrath's phial is Ashvein.

facts.label: Duvash received three consignment letters
facts.desc: Three separate consignments of old objects, Stormwatch origin, all moving north — Yngondi direction. Months apart.

facts.label: Kohlmar — name on one of Duvash's letters
facts.desc: A courier name that appears on two of the three letters. Not a Torweg name. Points north.

facts.label: Assassins sent for Duvash — Vane's authority
facts.desc: Two men with the Mathr household mark tried to reach Duvash two nights ago. He hid. He knows why.

facts.label: Mathr's family — no prior documentation anywhere
facts.desc: Brynja checked every archive she could reach. Nothing before sixty years ago. The family simply appears.

facts.label: Vane's family elevated by Mathr — sixty years ago
facts.desc: Vane's grandfather was elevated to under-chieftain by Mathr in the same year the Mathr family appears in the record.

facts.label: Mathr's service — sixty years, unchanged
facts.desc: Mathr has held his council seat for sixty years. He does not appear to have aged. Vane has never done this arithmetic.

facts.label: Token belongs to Mathr — not Vane
facts.desc: The token from the Halvash end-fight is Jarl Mathr's personal token. Vane does not own it. He acts as Mathr's hand.

facts.label: Third letter — Vane's name, Mathr's authority
facts.desc: The third letter from Duvash's set names Vane explicitly — and cites Mathr's household seal as authority for the movement.

facts.label: Vane realises — the token is not his to give
facts.desc: The moment Vane looks at the token and understands it is not his. A loyal man confronts the first evidence of the thing he served.

facts.label: Vane stands down — not an arrest
facts.desc: Vane chooses not to fight. Either helps the party or simply stops. He is not the target.

facts.label: Truthspeaker arrives — sent by Beorn
facts.desc: Beorn sent a Truthspeaker to Torweg without knowing why he felt uneasy. She doesn't know why she was sent either. Yet.

facts.label: Ashvein pin — recognised by the Truthspeaker
facts.desc: The Truthspeaker recognises the Ashvein pin in the south dock cache. Connects it to the Halvash geology. Xyrrath's phial is the same material.

facts.label: Operation is Mathr's — the chain is complete
facts.desc: The full chain: Mathr → Vane → south dock → Halvash Shadow-Walker passage → Pryzi vault. Evidence is enough to take to Beorn.

facts.label: Brynja hands over the full record
facts.desc: Brynja gives the party everything she has — once she knows they have enough to use it safely.

facts.label: Dugweb visits in seven weeks — the clock
facts.desc: Seven weeks until Dugweb's annual visit. If the party can reach Beorn with evidence before then, Dugweb's authority makes the case unassailable.
